agent.root_goal: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.
agent.task: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.

action.reasoning: The task of building a full-stack todo app is complex and naturally decomposes into four distinct phases: schema design, backend implementation, frontend implementation, and integration testing. These phases have clear dependencies: the backend depends on the schema, the frontend depends on the backend API, and the tests depend on both. I will spawn subtasks for each phase to allow for parallel work where possible (e.g., schema design can start immediately, backend can start once schema is done, etc.) and ensure each subtask has a clear goal and output. I will take the self_role of integrating the final deliverables and ensuring the overall system works.

subtasks.goal: Design the database schema for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask defines the data model for todos, including fields like id, title, description, completed status, and timestamps. It serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the foundational structure for the backend and frontend.

outputs.description: JSON or SQL definition of the todo app database schema.

subtasks.goal: Implement the backend API for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask builds the server-side logic, including CRUD endpoints for todos, based on the schema. It serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the API that the frontend will consume.

outputs.description: Source code for the backend API (e.g., Node.js/Express, Python/FastAPI).
API specification (e.g., OpenAPI/Swagger) or endpoint documentation.

subtasks.goal: Implement the frontend for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask builds the user interface, including components for listing, adding, editing, and deleting todos, interacting with the backend API. It serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the user-facing part of the application.

outputs.description: Source code for the frontend (e.g., React, Vue, or plain HTML/JS).

subtasks.goal: Write integration tests for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask creates tests that verify the interaction between the frontend and backend, ensuring the full-stack app works correctly. It serves the ROOT GOAL by validating the integrated system.

outputs.description: Test code and scripts for integration testing.

self_role.goal: Integrate the outputs from all subtasks and provide a final summary of the full-stack todo app.

outputs.description: A summary document describing the completed full-stack todo app, including links to schema, backend, frontend, and tests.

self_role.condition: done("root.1/schema") AND done("root.2/backend_code") AND done("root.3/frontend_code") AND done("root.4/integration_tests")

action.reasoning: The task is to implement a backend API based on a provided SQL schema. This is a standard, well-defined programming task that can be completed in a single focused effort. The schema is available in shared memory, so no data is missing. Spawning subtasks for such a straightforward implementation would add unnecessary overhead and complexity. Therefore, I will execute the task directly, producing both the backend code and the API specification.

result_outputs.description: Source code for the backend API using Node.js and Express, including CRUD endpoints for todos.
OpenAPI 3.0 specification documenting the todo API endpoints.

agent.root_goal: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.
agent.task: Write integration tests for the todo app.
agent.capsule: This subtask creates tests that verify the interaction between the frontend and backend, ensuring the full-stack app works correctly. It serves the ROOT GOAL by validating the integrated system.

expected_outputs.description: Test code and scripts for integration testing.

action.reasoning: The task is to write integration tests for the full-stack todo app. I have access to the backend code (FastAPI) and frontend code (HTML/JS) in shared memory. Integration tests should verify the API endpoints work correctly and that the frontend can interact with the backend. Since the backend is a FastAPI app, I can use `httpx` for async testing. The frontend is a static HTML file, so true end-to-end browser testing would require a browser automation tool like Playwright or Selenium, which is complex to set up in this context. A more practical approach for 'integration tests' in this context is to test the API endpoints thoroughly (which is the integration point between frontend and backend) and potentially mock the frontend interactions. I will write a comprehensive test suite using `pytest` and `httpx` that tests all CRUD operations on the Todo API, ensuring the backend behaves as expected for the frontend to consume. This covers the critical integration path.

result_outputs.description: Python test file using pytest and httpx to test the FastAPI backend endpoints, simulating frontend interactions.
